evidence_quotes.quote: increase prices without a fair notice
plans that lock you into very longterm agreements

paragraphs.text: Hello, I want to state my case against auto-renew subscription services, like those provided by video streaming services, video games, gyms, and most software these days. I have experienced online services that make it increasingly difficult to cancel their subscriptions or provide only plans that lock you into very longterm agreements. They also increase prices without a fair notice and give you no means to opt out of this price increase, they automatically continue your membership even if they have increased the price. My recent personal experience was with Netflix, they increased the price of the service and automatically kept me subscribed even if I didn't agree to the new price. When I tried to cancel, the cancelling process was very difficult. The cancel page was hidden, and had multiple "confirmations" before your account was "ready" for cancelling, but not actually cancelled. It was still active for the period until your next supposed payment. The problem is that within this "ready for cancelling" period, they would constantly notify me to renew, which was only a confirm button that instantly resumed my plan. My mother also uses Netflix, and she is not at all technical, she clicked the "confirm" button multiple times without understanding what she pressed, leading to me taking 3 months to successfully cancel my Netflix plan. They also do not allow you to remove your payment method unless you add a new one, so there was no simple way to stop their payments from their own platform. Another abusive systems seems to be with gym memberships, everything is online now and they only provide multiple month plans that auto renew. I believe auto-renew subscriptions should be an exception, and only explicitly requested by a customer, not the forced option without alternatives. Thank you!
